title: chelone glabra subsp. dilatata
intro: Chelone glabra subsp. dilatata, also known as the white turtlehead, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the balm family (Lamiaceae) that is native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Chelone glabra subsp. dilatata include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from colds and flu to sore throats and digestive problems.
